desc: BadTV scan-line brightness CORRECTED (2026-07-23): it is a PURE CODE-SPACE MULTIPLY with the correct band polarity — bright band = input*ScanBright, dark band = IDENTITY. The earlier gamma model was fit to a CORRUPTED horizontal-block-ramp probe and was WRONG.
model: factor = mix(1.0, ScanBright, smoothstep(band));  out = in * factor  (code space). dark band=identity, bright band=*ScanBright.
bug_fixed: The prior scanlineFactor returned scanBright+(1-scanBright)*ss = INVERTED bands (dark band got *sb, bright band got *1.0). flat-gray-128 coincidentally matched (128*1.5=192 either way at the midpoint) hiding the bug. Corrected to 1+(scanBright-1)*ss.
textured_synth_confound: On the TEXTURED synth, headless brightens dark_bg 40->133 (not the flat-40 result of 61) — a content-dependent effect (likely a Mix/composite or frame-adaptive op in the synth render path), NOT the scan-line transfer. The textured-synth PSNR is therefore an unreliable metric for BadTV; the clean FLAT-value probe (matches to ~1 level) is authoritative.

content_dependence.finding: BadTV scan-line BRIGHTNESS is CONTENT-DEPENDENT (spatial/adaptive), not purely per-pixel. Same input value 40 at sb=1.5 gives headless 61 on a FLAT-40 frame but 133 on the textured synth (surrounded by the bright disc/gradient). At sb=1.0 both are identity (MAD 1.1), ruling out a Mix/composite confound — the effect is in the scanline brightness itself.
content_dependence.implication: The bright-band lift scales with a LOCAL or GLOBAL average luma (auto-exposure-like), so a per-pixel in*ScanBright model is exact ONLY for flat/low-variance content. The textured-synth PSNR (~11 dB) reflects this adaptive behavior, not a per-pixel decode error. Fully matching needs decoding the (local/global) luma statistic that modulates ScanBright — a deeper spatial probe (vary surround brightness, measure the lift).
content_dependence.validated_so_far: band polarity (bright=*ScanBright, dark=identity) + the per-pixel multiply are correct on flat inputs (~1 level). The content-adaptive brightness gain is the remaining, separately-scoped piece.
content_dependence.step_probe: Surround-brightness probe (fixed dark patch=40, right half=B): bright-band output is 61 (=40*1.5, per-pixel model) for B in {40,80,128}, then STEPS to 133 for B in {180,255}. NOT a smooth global-mean scale -- a THRESHOLD/step adaptation (kicks in when enough of the frame exceeds ~a bright threshold). This is a complex auto-brightness/tone behavior beyond a simple per-pixel or global-avg model; decoding it fully needs a dense 2-D (surround-brightness x fraction) probe + likely the HgcBadTV P-buffer luma-stat register trace. Band-polarity fix (bright=*ScanBright, dark=identity) remains correct + validated on flat content; the adaptive gain is a separately-scoped deep probe with diminishing ROI vs other coverage.
